hook.text: Where rivers meet the ocean, freshwater and saltwater mix in one of Earth's richest habitats.

buildup.text: Estuaries trap nutrients from both sources, fueling plankton blooms and dense food webs.

discovery.text: Most commercially harvested fish spend part of their lives in estuaries.

twist.text: Estuaries also trap pollutants. Urban and agricultural runoff concentrates in these mixing zones.

climax.text: Protecting estuaries means managing entire watersheds—what happens upstream arrives downstream.

punchline.text: Estuaries are where the river's story meets the ocean.

quiz.question: Why are estuaries so ecologically productive?
options: They trap nutrients from rivers and oceans
They are the deepest water bodies
